name: Frank Wess
description: American saxophonist, flutist, composer and arranger
abstract: Frank Wellington Wess (January 4, 1922 – October 30, 2013) was an American jazz saxophonist and flutist. He was renowned for his extensive solo work; however, he was also remembered for his time playing with Count Basie's band during the early 1950s into the early 1960s.

quotes.text: Well I tried to make sure that the sound was right before we started. So I went out in the room to hear myself, because that’s another thing, you can’t trust sound men. You can not. I don’t care where they are — Carnegie Hall or anyplace else. You can not trust them because they—
quotes.caption: General comment on sound engineers at Carnegie Hall
quotes.doc_title: Frank Wess interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 29, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Scott Robinson
about_quotes.evidence: He’s started an octet with two saxophones, just him and myself... I’m very honored to be asked by such a great writer to write, and by such a great saxophone player to be the other saxophone in his band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Scott Robinson interviewed by Monk Rowe,Chautauqua, New York, September 13, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Mitchell
about_quotes.evidence: Frank Wess and Frank Foster. ... The saxophone section was Charlie Fowlkes on baritone, Frank Wess playing alto and tenor, Frank Foster and myself playing tenor, and Marshall of course.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Mitchell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: Basie motioned for Frank Wess and Frank Foster to come forward. Wess played flute and Foster tenor on the two numbers I did with the band

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: There's also a version of it on a Savoy record which has Milt Jackson and Frank Wess.

about_quotes.speaker: Holly Hofmann
about_quotes.evidence: he suggested that I come to New York during the summers and study with Frank a bit... And he was also a teacher that was very strict about certain things and gave me a lot of input
about_quotes.doc_title: Holly Hofmann interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 13, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: John Malachi
about_quotes.evidence: he had this quartet that had Frank Wess on tenor saxophone... and of course Al Dunn on drums. During that time... I was sort of a supper club type place -- with a bandleader.
